description: capital and largest city of Japan
description_wiki: Tokyo, officially the Tokyo Metropolis, is the capital and most populous city of Japan.

description: Higher education institution in Chiba Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Chiba University (千葉大学, Chiba Daigaku) is a national university with campuses located in Chiba Prefecture, a region noted for its industrial, intellectual and international achievements.

description: samurai of the Bakumatsu period (1836–1888)
description_wiki: Yamaoka Tesshū (山岡 鉄舟; June 10, 1836 – July 19, 1888), also known as Ono Tetsutarō or Yamaoka Tetsutarō, was a famous samurai of the Bakumatsu period, who played an important role in the Meiji Restoration.
The Presbyterian Church in Japan is a conservative Reformed denomination in Japan, founded by American missionaries in the mid-1900s.

aliases: The Pilgrim’s Progress from This World to That Which is to Come
description: 1678 Christian allegory written by John Bunyan
description_wiki: The Pilgrim's Progress from This World, to That Which Is to Come is a 1678 Christian allegory written by the English nonconformist preacher John Bunyan.

description: collection of sacred books in Judaism and Christianity
description_wiki: The Bible is a collection of religious texts that are central to Christianity and Judaism, and esteemed in other Abrahamic religions such as Islam.

description: dissolved municipality in Chiba district, Chiba prefecture, Japan
city in Chiba prefecture, Japan
city in Chiba Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Kisarazu (木更津市, Kisarazu-shi) is a city located in Chiba Prefecture, Japan.

description: conflict between the Russian and Japanese empires from 1904 to 1905
description_wiki: The Russo-Japanese War (8 February 1904 – 5 September 1905) was fought between the Russian Empire and the Empire of Japan over rival imperial ambitions in Manchuria and the Korean Empire.

description: a polytheistic and animistic religion originating in Japan
description_wiki: Shinto (神道, Shintō; Japanese pronunciation: [ɕiꜜn.toː]), also called Shintoism, is the native religion and former ethnic cult of Japan.

description: Indian religion
description_wiki: Buddhism, also known as Buddha Dharma and Dharmavinaya, is an Indian religion and philosophy based on teachings attributed to the Buddha, a śramaṇa and religious teacher who lived in the 6th or 5th century BCE. It is the world's fourth-largest religion, with about 320 million followers, known as Buddhists, who comprise 4.1% of the global population.

description: Japanese private university
description_wiki: Meiji Gakuin University (明治学院大学, Meiji gakuin daigaku) is a private, Christian university with the main campus in Minato, Tokyo, Japan, and a satellite campus in Yokohama, Kanagawa.

description: island of Japan
description_wiki: Hokkaido is the second-largest and northernmost of Japan's four main islands.

description: overview of the role of Christianity in Japan
description_wiki: Christianity in Japan is among the nation's largest minority religions in terms of individuals who state an explicit affiliation or faith.

description: two or three-wheeled passenger cart, of Asian origin
description_wiki: Rickshaw originally denoted a pulled rickshaw, a two- or three-wheeled cart generally pulled by one person carrying one passenger.

description: a Japanese palanquin (enclosed litter) used for ceremonial transport of a Shinto deity
description_wiki: A mikoshi (Japanese: 神輿) is a sacred religious palanquin (also translated as portable Shinto shrine).

description: Japanese writer (1861–1930)
description_wiki: Uchimura Kanzō (内村 鑑三; March 26, 1861 – March 28, 1930) was a Japanese author, Christian evangelist, and the founder of the Nonchurch Movement (Mukyōkai) of Christianity during the Meiji and Taishō periods in Japan.

description: Japanese academic publishing company
churches which distance themselves from the confessionalism or creedalism of other Christian communities

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: Japanese family name (五十嵐)
description_wiki: Igarashi, Ikarashi or Isoarashi (五十嵐; "fifty storms") is a Japanese surname.

description: Japanese politician (1866-1945)
President of Doshisha University, 1907-1919
description_wiki: Tasuku Harada(原田 助) (December 20, 1863 – February 21, 1940) was a Japanese pastor and the president of Doshisha University from 1907 to 1919.

description: national university located in Kyoto, Japan
description_wiki: Kyoto University (京都大学, Kyōto daigaku), or KyotoU (京大, Kyōdai), is a national research university in Kyoto, Japan.

description: college and university system in the U.S. state of Hawaiʻi
Japanese family name (三森)
description_wiki: Mitsumori is a Japanese surname, masculine given name, and toponym.

description: city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Pasadena (  PAS-ə-DEE-nə) is a city in Los Angeles County, California, United States, 10 miles (16 km) northeast of downtown Los Angeles.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: capital city of the U.S. state of California and seat of Sacramento County
description_wiki: Sacramento is the capital city of the U.S. state of California.

description: evangelical Christian movement
ship built in 1972
private university in Tokyo, Japan
description_wiki: Waseda University (Japanese: 早稲田大学), abbreviated as Waseda (早稲田) or Sōdai (早大), is a private research university in Shinjuku, Tokyo.

description: American concentration camp
description_wiki: The Heart Mountain War Relocation Center, named after nearby Heart Mountain and located midway between the northwest Wyoming towns of Cody and Powell, was one of ten concentration camps used for the internment of Japanese Americans evicted during World War II from their local communities (including their homes, businesses, and college residencies) in the West Coast Exclusion Zone by the executive order of President Franklin Roosevelt (after the bombing of Pearl Harbor in December 1941, upon the recommendation of Lieutenant General John L. DeWitt).

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.
